mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, directly boosting S&P 500 valuations over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no statistical correlation with FEDFUNDS changes over a 5-year walk-forward test, or Rank IC remains below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 4.0 percent, labor market slack signals impending economic deceleration, prompting equity risk premium repricing and negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55 percent of instances where UNRATE exceeds 4.0 percent, invalidating the risk-off signal.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous contraction in real GDP and retail sales indicates a broad-based demand shock that compresses corporate earnings expectations, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the following quarter.
failure_condition: Joint declines in GDPC1 and RSAFS fail to predict negative 60-day forward returns, with out-of-sample accuracy dropping below 45 percent across rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production during a late-cycle expansion reflects resilient manufacturing output and capacity utilization, generating short-term momentum and positive S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases during late-cycle phases yield negative 5-day forward returns in over 60 percent of cases, contradicting the momentum mechanism.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 2.5 percent, restrictive monetary policy dampens economic growth and equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns remain positive in more than 50 percent of periods where FEDFUNDS exceeds 2.5 percent, negating the tightening penalty.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in total nonfarm payrolls and a declining unemployment rate confirm robust labor market tightening, supporting consumer spending and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal of rising PAYEMS and falling UNRATE fails to predict positive 20-day returns, with out-of-sample hit rate below 48 percent over rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Increases in housing starts signal expanding residential investment and broader economic confidence, which translates into higher corporate earnings expectations and positive S&P 500 returns over the following quarter.
failure_condition: HOUST increases show no predictive power for 60-day forward returns, with Rank IC consistently below 0.01 across all tested rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in industrial production below 105 during a post-shock recovery phase indicates fragile supply-side momentum, triggering risk aversion and negative S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 generates positive 5-day returns in more than 55 percent of instances where INDPRO falls below 105 during recovery phases, invalidating the fragility signal.
